senses.definition: a piece of writing that briefly analyzes or discusses a specific subject
a written piece of work that expresses thoughts, ideas, arguments, or analyses on a particular topic or subject

examples.x: Her composition explored the theme of identity in contemporary literature, drawing on various literary works as examples.
The student's composition on environmental conservation highlighted the importance of sustainable practices and community involvement.
The composition contest challenged participants to craft persuasive arguments on pressing social issues.
The teacher provided feedback on the students' compositions, focusing on structure, coherence, and clarity of ideas.
In his composition, the author provided a critical analysis of the political implications of globalization.

senses.definition: an official document written by a group or an individual who studied a particular subject, containing the findings
an additional academic opportunity offered to students to improve their grades by completing supplementary assignments or tasks beyond the regular requirements

examples.x: The teacher allowed students to earn extra credit by participating in a community service project.
Sarah completed the optional research paper for extra credit to boost her overall grade in the class.
John attended the guest lecture for extra credit points towards his final grade.
The professor offered extra credit opportunities for students who attended study sessions outside of class.
The biology teacher gave extra credit to students who conducted additional experiments beyond the required lab assignments.

senses.definition: a particular task involving careful study of a subject, done by school or college students

examples.x: In the geography project, the students made a map of different countries and their capitals.
Jane's project on endangered species won first place in the school competition.
The art project involved painting a beautiful landscape using watercolors.
The English project required the students to write a short story and present it to the class.
The students presented their science project on renewable energy sources.

senses.definition: a piece of work for someone to do, especially as an assignment

examples.x: The teacher assigned a reading task to the students for homework.
He completed the task ahead of schedule and earned extra points.
Cleaning the garage was a daunting task, but they managed to finish it.
She was given the task of organizing the company event.
The manager delegated the task to her most trusted employee.

senses.definition: a series of questions in a book set to test one's knowledge or skill

examples.x: The math textbook included a variety of exercises at the end of each chapter to help students practice solving equations.
The language workbook contained exercises that focused on vocabulary, grammar, and comprehension to improve students' language skills.
As part of the science curriculum, students were assigned weekly lab exercises to conduct experiments and analyze results.

senses.definition: the academic tasks, assignments, or activities assigned to students by teachers or educational institutions

examples.x: She spent all afternoon finishing her schoolwork.
His schoolwork involves reading chapters and completing exercises.
The teacher collected the schoolwork at the end of the week.
They had to complete their schoolwork before they could go outside.
He used a planner to organize his schoolwork.

senses.definition: tasks that are given to students to do in class, and not at home

examples.x: The teacher assigned a group discussion as part of the classwork for today.
Students were asked to solve math problems on the board for their classwork.
Completing the worksheet was part of the classwork for the lesson on fractions.
The classwork involved conducting experiments in the science laboratory.
Completing the reading comprehension exercises was part of the daily classwork.

senses.definition: schoolwork that students have to do at home

examples.x: I always double-check my homework to ensure it is accurate.
I have a lot of homework to complete tonight.
My daughter spends a few hours every evening doing her homework.
They need to finish their English homework before the deadline.
We use textbooks and online resources to help us with our homework.

senses.definition: a long piece of writing on a particular subject that a university student presents in order to get an advanced degree

examples.x: She spent months researching for her dissertation on climate change.
His dissertation was praised for its thorough analysis and originality.
Writing the dissertation was challenging but ultimately rewarding.
Her dissertation focused on the impact of technology in education.
The university requires students to defend their dissertation before a committee.

senses.definition: a collaborative assignment in which multiple individuals work together to achieve a common goal or complete a task

examples.x: The teacher assigned a group project to encourage teamwork and collaboration among students.
Sarah's group project focused on researching environmental sustainability practices in their community.
John's team divided the tasks evenly to complete their group project on time.
The group project required students to present their findings to the class at the end of the semester.
The company's new initiative involved employees working on a group project to improve workplace efficiency.

senses.definition: a final assignment where students use what they have learned to complete a big project or solve a problem

examples.x: A student designed a computer program to optimize hospital resource allocation for their capstone project.
The engineering team constructed a prototype solar-powered vehicle as their capstone project.
For her capstone project, Sarah conducted a comprehensive study on the effects of climate change on local ecosystems.
The business students developed a strategic marketing plan for a startup company as part of their capstone project.
As his capstone project, John collaborated with a local nonprofit organization to implement a community outreach program aimed at tackling homelessness.

senses.definition: a brief document outlining the main ideas or proposals for a project or research study

examples.x: before starting the full proposal, the research team drafted a concept paper to outline their study objectives.
the concept paper provided a clear roadmap for the development of the new product.
the committee reviewed several concept papers before selecting the most promising project for funding.
as part of the grant application process, applicants were required to submit a concept paper detailing their proposed initiatives.
the concept paper served as a foundation for discussion during the project planning meeting.

senses.definition: a scholarly document presenting findings from an investigation or study on a particular topic

examples.x: The professor assigned a research paper on the effects of climate change on biodiversity.
Sarah spent weeks gathering data for her research paper on the history of feminism in America.
The conference accepted John's research paper, which explored the relationship between social media use and mental health.
The student's research paper delved into the ethical implications of artificial intelligence in healthcare.
Dr. Smith's groundbreaking research paper on quantum computing was published in a prestigious scientific journal.

senses.definition: a written piece that expresses the writer's thoughts and insights on a particular topic or experience

examples.x: After the field trip, the students wrote reflection papers on their observations and learnings.
Writing a reflection paper on the novel helped me deepen my understanding of its themes.
The workshop encouraged participants to write reflection papers on their personal growth throughout the program.
Each student presented their reflection paper during the final class session, sharing their reflections on the semester's material.
The therapist asked her clients to write reflection papers as part of their self-discovery process.

senses.definition: an original piece of writing on a particular subject that a candidate for a university degree presents based on their research

examples.x: She spent months conducting experiments and analyzing data for her thesis, which was an essential part of her university degree in chemistry.
Completing a thesis is a significant requirement for earning a university degree in most academic disciplines.
The doctoral candidate defended her thesis on quantum computing, presenting groundbreaking research that advances the field's understanding of quantum algorithms.

senses.definition: a recorded analysis of a person, group, event or situation over a length of time

examples.x: Researchers conducted a case study to explore the long-term effects of a new drug on patients with rare medical conditions.
The psychology professor assigned a case study on a famous psychological disorder to illustrate its symptoms and treatment options.
The case study examined how a small business adapted to changes in consumer behavior during the pandemic.
The marketing team presented a case study on a successful social media campaign to demonstrate its impact on brand awareness.
The environmentalist conducted a case study on the effects of deforestation on local wildlife populations.
